title: EU-Japan-South Korea Cooperation Is Indispensable Now
gist: South Korea's postwar strategy of anchoring foreign policy to a stable US alliance is breaking down as Washington bundles tariffs, defense procurement and alliance politics into a single transactional package that treats allies like Japan and the EU no differently from rivals.

themes.theme: Erosion of the US alliance anchor

insights.claim: For decades South Korea's foreign policy strategy rested on treating the US alliance as a stable security anchor around which it managed relations with Japan, Europe, China, and regional institutions, but Seoul can no longer assume this foundation will stay stable.
insights.speaker: Gi-Wook Shin
insights.so_what: Investors modeling South Korean policy continuity should discount assumptions of a fixed, low-risk US security guarantee and price in greater unpredictability in Seoul's alignment choices.
insights.locator: For decades, South Korean strategy rested on a stable hierarchy within the international order.

insights.claim: In 2025 the United States initially announced sharply differentiated 'reciprocal' tariffs on South Korea, Japan, and the European Union before negotiations produced a common 15 percent baseline tariff framework for all three.
insights.speaker: Gi-Wook Shin
insights.so_what: A 15 percent baseline tariff across three major US allies is a durable cost floor for trade-exposed South Korean, Japanese, and EU exporters to the US market.
insights.locator: In 2025, the United States initially announced sharply differentiated

insights.claim: The US now handles trade access, industrial investment, defense procurement, and alliance politics with South Korea, Japan, and the EU as one bundled bargaining package, meaning allied status no longer exempts a country from economic pressure.
insights.speaker: Gi-Wook Shin
insights.so_what: Expect future US trade and defense negotiations with allies to be linked rather than siloed, raising the stakes of any single-issue dispute (e.g., tariffs) for the whole relationship.
insights.quote: Allies were not exempt from economic pressure because they were allies.
insights.locator: Trade access, industrial investment, defense procurement, and alliance politics

themes.theme: Case for trilateral EU-Japan-South Korea cooperation

insights.claim: The author argues that closer cooperation among the EU, Japan, and South Korea has become indispensable as a hedge against a less predictable US role in the alliance structure.
insights.speaker: Gi-Wook Shin
insights.so_what: Watch for incremental EU-Japan-South Korea coordination on trade and industrial policy as a diversification play away from single-point US dependence.
insights.locator: headline framing: EU-Japan-South Korea Cooperation Is Indispensable Now
